evals.prompt: I want to add BarcodeCapture to my .NET MAUI app. Here's my empty page: EmptyMainPage.xaml + EmptyMainPage.xaml.cs, and my MauiProgram: EmptyMauiProgram.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and Code 128 barcodes for a retail app.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit MAUI NuGet versions, updates MauiProgram.cs with .UseScanditCore(c => c.AddDataCaptureView()).UseScanditBarcode(), adds <scandit:DataCaptureView> to MainPage.xaml with the correct namespace, writes complete BarcodeCapture integration into MainPage.xaml.cs (BarcodeCapture.Create, BarcodeScanned event or IBarcodeCaptureListener, BarcodeCaptureOverlay created in HandlerChanged, OnAppearing/OnDisappearing lifecycle, Permissions.Camera). Shows the setup checklist (four NuGet packages, NSCameraUsageDescription on iOS, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages: Scandit.DataCapture.Core, Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Maui, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode, Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Maui
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditCore(
MauiProgram.cs UseScanditCore call invokes configure.AddDataCaptureView()
MauiProgram.cs is updated to call .UseScanditBarcode()
MauiProgram.cs does NOT call .UseScanditBarcode( with an inner configure argument (e.g. AddBarcodeCaptureView)
MainPage.xaml adds the xmlns:scandit="clr-namespace:Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Maui;assembly=ScanditCaptureCoreMaui" namespace
MainPage.xaml contains a <scandit:DataCaptureView> element
<scandit:DataCaptureView> binds its DataCaptureContext property (via {Binding ...} or x:Name + code-behind)
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Platforms/iOS/Info.plist
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
BarcodeCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings is used (as a property, not VideoResolution.Auto)
Camera.GetCamera(CameraPosition.WorldFacing) or Camera.GetDefaultCamera() is used to obtain the camera
camera.ApplySettingsAsync(BarcodeCapture.RecommendedCameraSettings) is called
BarcodeCaptureSettings.Create() is used (not new BarcodeCaptureSettings())
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Code128 is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Code128 are enabled
BarcodeCapture.Create(dataCaptureContext, settings) is used to create the mode
barcodeCapture.BarcodeScanned += handler is wired (or barcodeCapture.AddListener if using IBarcodeCaptureListener)
dataCaptureView.HandlerChanged is subscribed to in the page code-behind
BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Create(barcodeCapture) is called inside the HandlerChanged handler (single-argument overload)
dataCaptureView.AddOverlay(overlay) is called to attach the overlay
OnAppearing is overridden and calls Permissions.CheckStatusAsync<Permissions.Camera>() or Permissions.RequestAsync<Permissions.Camera>()
OnAppearing turns the camera on (SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.On))
OnDisappearing turns the camera off (SwitchToDesiredStateAsync(FrameSourceState.Off))
barcodeCapture.Enabled = false is set inside the BarcodeScanned event handler (or OnBarcodeScanned)
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is used to dispatch UI work (not RunOnUiThread, not DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync)

evals.prompt: Add barcode scanning to my existing .NET MAUI page. Here's the page: EmptyMainPage.xaml + EmptyMainPage.xaml.cs and MauiProgram: EmptyMauiProgram.cs. I want to scan QR codes and Data Matrix. When a barcode is scanned, show a DisplayAlert with the value.
evals.expected_output: The skill integrates BarcodeCapture into the page with QR and Data Matrix enabled, shows a DisplayAlert in the BarcodeScanned handler (dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread), updates MauiProgram.cs with the Scandit builder chain, and shows the setup checklist.

assertions.text: Symbology.Qr is enabled
Symbology.DataMatrix is enabled
No symbologies other than Qr and DataMatrix are enabled
BarcodeCapture.Create( is used
The BarcodeScanned event handler (or OnBarcodeScanned) reads args.Session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode or session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode
DisplayAlert is called with barcode.Data (or equivalent shown UI feedback)
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is used to dispatch the DisplayAlert
MauiProgram.cs calls .UseScanditCore and .UseScanditBarcode()
Setup checklist mentions all four NuGet packages

evals.prompt: When a barcode is scanned in my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture app, I want to disable scanning, look up the barcode in a remote API (an async Task call), and re-enable scanning when the lookup completes. Wire this up safely.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeCapture.Enabled = false at the start of the BarcodeScanned handler, awaits the async lookup, and re-enables scanning in a finally block. UI updates are dispatched via MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread.

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.Enabled = false is set inside the BarcodeScanned handler before the async work
An async/await call or a Task continuation is used to perform the lookup
barcodeCapture.Enabled = true is set after the lookup completes (typically in a finally block)
MainThread.BeginInvokeOnMainThread is used to dispatch UI work after the async lookup
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration when a barcode is scanned in my .NET MAUI app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeCapture feedback.
evals.expected_output: The skill reassigns barcodeCapture.Feedback.Success to a Feedback constructed with null vibration and null sound (or replaces the whole BarcodeCaptureFeedback with one whose Success has no audio or haptic effect).

assertions.text: barcodeCapture.Feedback.Success is assigned to a Feedback with no sound and no vibration (Feedback(vibration: null, sound: null) or equivalent)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a square viewfinder to my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture preview so users can see where to aim.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a RectangularViewfinder with RectangularViewfinderStyle.Square and assigns it to overlay.Viewfinder inside the HandlerChanged handler (so it is set after the overlay is created).

assertions.text: A RectangularViewfinder is constructed with RectangularViewfinderStyle.Square
The viewfinder is assigned to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Viewfinder (overlay.Viewfinder = ...)
The viewfinder assignment happens inside the HandlerChanged handler (i.e. after the overlay has been created)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to filter scans so duplicates of the same code within 500 milliseconds are ignored. Update my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets settings.CodeDuplicateFilter = TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) on the BarcodeCaptureSettings instance (using TimeSpan, not a bare integer, double, or TimeInterval).

assertions.text: CodeDuplicateFilter is set to TimeSpan.FromMilliseconds(500) (not a bare integer 500, not a double, not TimeInterval)
CodeDuplicateFilter is set before BarcodeCapture.Create( or via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: Add an aimer viewfinder to my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture preview so users have a single aim point to line up the code, instead of a rectangular frame.
evals.expected_output: The skill constructs an AimerViewfinder (from Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Viewfinder) and assigns it to overlay.Viewfinder inside the HandlerChanged handler, after the overlay has been created. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: An AimerViewfinder is constructed with new AimerViewfinder()
The viewfinder is assigned to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Viewfinder (overlay.Viewfinder = ...)
The AimerViewfinder assignment happens inside the HandlerChanged handler (i.e. after the overlay has been created)
RectangularViewfinder is NOT used (the user asked for an aimer, not a rectangular frame)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: I only want to scan barcodes that appear inside a centered horizontal strip in the middle of my .NET MAUI preview — about 90% of the width and 30% of the height. Codes outside that band should be ignored.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets BarcodeCaptureSettings.LocationSelection to a RectangularLocationSelection.Create(...) built from a SizeWithUnit whose width and height are FloatWithUnit values in MeasureUnit.Fraction (0.9 wide, 0.3 tall). The location selection is applied to the settings before BarcodeCapture.Create or via ApplySettingsAsync. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.LocationSelection is assigned a RectangularLocationSelection.Create( instance
The size is built with new SizeWithUnit( taking two FloatWithUnit arguments
The width and height FloatWithUnit values use MeasureUnit.Fraction
The fraction values are 0.9f for width and 0.3f for height
LocationSelection is set on the settings before BarcodeCapture.Create( or applied via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture app I want to restrict scanning to a circular area around the center of the preview, with a radius of 20% of the smaller view dimension.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets BarcodeCaptureSettings.LocationSelection to a RadiusLocationSelection.Create(...) built from a FloatWithUnit radius in MeasureUnit.Fraction (0.2). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.LocationSelection is assigned a RadiusLocationSelection.Create( instance
The radius is built with new FloatWithUnit( using MeasureUnit.Fraction
The radius fraction value is 0.2f
LocationSelection is set on the settings before BarcodeCapture.Create( or applied via barcodeCapture.ApplySettingsAsync
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture app, our valid product codes always start with the prefix "PRD-". When a scanned code does not start with that prefix, I want to ignore it and keep scanning instead of acting on it. Wire up this rejection logic.
evals.expected_output: Inside the BarcodeScanned handler the skill inspects barcode.Data, and when it does not match the accepted pattern it returns early WITHOUT disabling scanning or acting on the result (so scanning continues). Accepted codes proceed to the normal handling (disable + process). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: The BarcodeScanned handler reads barcode.Data from args.Session.NewlyRecognizedBarcode
barcode.Data is checked against the "PRD-" prefix (e.g. barcode.Data.StartsWith("PRD-"))
When the code does NOT match, the handler returns early without acting on the result (semantic)
On rejection scanning is NOT disabled, so barcodeCapture.Enabled stays true and scanning continues (semantic)
For an accepted code, barcodeCapture.Enabled = false is set before the result is processed
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: I want to hide the default blue highlight that Scandit draws over recognized barcodes in my .NET MAUI preview — the overlay should not visibly mark scanned codes at all.
evals.expected_output: The skill assigns Brush.TransparentBrush to the BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Brush property inside the HandlerChanged handler (after the overlay is created). Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Brush is assigned Brush.TransparentBrush (overlay.Brush = Brush.TransparentBrush)
The Brush assignment happens inside the HandlerChanged handler (i.e. after the overlay has been created)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: Customize the recognized-barcode highlight in my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture preview to use a semi-transparent green fill with a solid green 2-pixel stroke instead of the default Scandit blue.
evals.expected_output: The skill creates a new Brush(fillColor, strokeColor, strokeWidth) with a green fill and stroke and a strokeWidth of 2, and assigns it to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Brush inside the HandlerChanged handler. The Brush type comes from Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Style. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: A new Brush( is constructed with three arguments (fill color, stroke color, stroke width)
The result is assigned to BarcodeCaptureOverlay.Brush (overlay.Brush = ...)
The stroke width argument is 2f (a float)
The fill and stroke colors are green (semantic)
The Brush assignment happens inside the HandlerChanged handler (i.e. after the overlay has been created)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: My .NET MAUI app scans Code 39 codes that contain the full ASCII character set (lowercase letters and symbols). Enable the full-ASCII extension for Code 39 in my BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables Code39, fetches its per-symbology settings via settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code39), and calls SetExtensionEnabled("full_ascii", true) on them. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: Symbology.Code39 is enabled via settings.EnableSymbology(Symbology.Code39, true)
settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code39) is called to obtain the SymbologySettings
SetExtensionEnabled("full_ascii", true) is called on the SymbologySettings
The extension name string is the literal "full_ascii"
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: Our Code 39 labels use a Mod 43 checksum. Configure my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture settings so the Mod 43 checksum is enforced for Code 39.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables Code39, fetches its per-symbology settings via settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code39), and sets Checksums = Checksum.Mod43 on them. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: Symbology.Code39 is enabled via settings.EnableSymbology(Symbology.Code39, true)
settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code39) is called to obtain the SymbologySettings
The Checksums property is assigned Checksum.Mod43 (symbologySettings.Checksums = Checksum.Mod43)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: My Code 128 codes are always between 7 and 10 symbols long. Restrict the active symbol counts for Code 128 in my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture settings to that range to cut down on misreads.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables Code128, fetches its per-symbology settings via settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code128), and assigns ActiveSymbolCounts a collection of short values covering 7 through 10. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code128) is called to obtain the SymbologySettings
The ActiveSymbolCounts property is assigned a collection of short values (symbologySettings.ActiveSymbolCounts = ...)
The assigned collection covers the symbol counts 7, 8, 9 and 10
The collection element type is short (e.g. new HashSet<short> or a short[] cast), not int
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: Some of our Code 128 labels are printed light-on-dark (color inverted). Enable scanning of color-inverted Code 128 codes in my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture settings.
evals.expected_output: The skill fetches the Code128 per-symbology settings via settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code128) and sets ColorInvertedEnabled = true on them. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: settings.GetSymbologySettings(Symbology.Code128) is called to obtain the SymbologySettings
The ColorInvertedEnabled property is set to true (symbologySettings.ColorInvertedEnabled = true)
The deprecated/nonexistent IsColorInvertedEnabled property name is NOT used; the .NET property is ColorInvertedEnabled (semantic)
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved

evals.prompt: I need to scan GS1 Composite codes (a linear EAN-13 with a 2D companion component) in my .NET MAUI BarcodeCapture app. Enable composite code scanning.
evals.expected_output: The skill enables the base symbologies (e.g. Ean13Upca), calls settings.EnableSymbologies(CompositeType...) to enable the symbologies required by the composite types, and sets settings.EnabledCompositeTypes to the desired CompositeType flags. Existing integration is preserved.

assertions.text: The base linear symbology is enabled (settings.EnableSymbology(Symbology.Ean13Upca, true))
settings.EnableSymbologies( is called with a CompositeType value (e.g. CompositeType.A or CompositeType.A | CompositeType.B)
settings.EnabledCompositeTypes is assigned the desired CompositeType flags
The CompositeType enum values used are members A, B and/or C
Existing integration code (DataCaptureContext, barcodeCapture, camera, HandlerChanged overlay creation) is preserved
